human_verification.notes: Reviewed the cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations package for cannabis workflow specificity, source quality, laboratory and seed-to-sale realism, hold and remediation boundary discipline, adjacent-lane routing, and validated spec-pack completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to cannabis-sector quality evidence review, seed-to-sale truth-set handling, COA and data-package review, hold and remediation boundary handling, and complaint or incident escalation.

Authority sources cover current California, Oregon, Massachusetts, Colorado, ASTM, AOAC, and FDA references that materially change terminology, artifacts, systems, and refusal posture for cannabis-quality work.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete evidence, repeat defects, control-bypass requests, industry-specific prerequisite gaps, unresolved holds, incident escalation, adjacent laboratory and legal routing, policy conflict, tenant adaptation, and attempts to close findings without review support.
The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/cannabis-and-controlled-substance-operations/cannabis-quality-specialist/ to preserve the canonical NAICS-derived overlay path.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around final release, remediation approval, laboratory signoff, legal interpretation, medical advice, and recall-command ownership.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated cannabis-sector quality overlay where final release, laboratory, legal, medical, recall, and regulator-facing authority remain external.
Runtime remains specification-based until a retained implementation is separately promoted and committed.
